scenario: FR-017 lineage + concurrency: (dedup) <=1 in-flight review per lineage; (out-of-order) an obsolete completion whose digest != current is superseded; (correction 5) a DETERMINISTIC persisted lineage id, and a MONOTONIC authority rule for SAME-DIGEST concurrent completions (exactly one authoritative = max run_id); (correction 1) the auto-fire baseline is the last-ACCEPTED reviewed TREE.
note: Lineage is keyed by the STABLE anchor (merge-base commit) + target — not the advancing baseline_tree_id and not the per-fire checkpoint_id. run_ids are fire-time-sortable (yyyyMMddTHHmmssfff-<hex>), so max run_id is a monotone authority. Contract predicates under test are PURE.

lineage_id_determinism.note: Get-ContinuousCoReviewLineageId(anchor,target) is deterministic (same inputs -> same id) and stable across the lineage; a different anchor or target yields a different id.

auto_fire_baseline.note: the next auto-fire diffs advance from the LAST ACCEPTED reviewed TREE (a tree-id), not a commit ref; git ancestry uses commit refs separately.

cases.name: dedup-second-fire-does-not-launch
cases.why: run1 is already in flight for this lineage; a Stop-fired review waits/polls it, never launches a duplicate.

cases.name: different-lineage-fire-may-launch
cases.why: no in-flight review exists for a DIFFERENT lineage.

cases.name: out-of-order-older-completion-superseded
cases.why: an older-digest completion (arriving out of order) never matches the current tree; superseded, never a fresh block or authorization.

cases.name: current-digest-completion-authoritative
cases.why: its reviewed digest equals the current tree (single-run case; ties resolved by same-digest authority below).

same_digest_concurrent.note: TWO terminal runs complete for the SAME current digest (a genuine concurrent race). Exactly one is authoritative by the monotonic rule = max run_id; the other is superseded. A third run at a stale digest is not eligible.
same_digest_concurrent.why: concurB has the max run_id among same-digest TERMINAL runs -> authoritative (monotone). concurA is superseded. staleC (wrong digest) and runningD (not terminal) are not eligible.
